meta.title: EU Tech Sovereignty Universe
meta.context: Companies that could form the EU's sovereign technology stack, mapped to the European Technological Sovereignty Package (adopted 3 June 2026: Chips Act 2.0, Cloud & AI Development Act, Open Source Strategy).
meta.fx_note: Listed market caps & multiples are point-in-time via yfinance/Yahoo Finance on 2026-06-05; non-EUR converted at EUR=USD 1.1585 / GBP 0.8633 / SEK 10.90 / NOK 10.88 / CHF 0.918. Revenues are latest reported FY (FY ends differ: Infineon=Sep, Soitec=Mar, Eutelsat=Jun). size_eur = market cap (listed) or last valuation / funding / revenue (private); null => default node size.
meta.disclaimer: Informational only. NOT investment advice. Figures are point-in-time and may be stale; verify live quotes and primary filings before any decision.

companies.name: ASML Holding
companies.description: The world's sole supplier of extreme-ultraviolet (EUV) lithography systems and a leader in deep-UV (DUV) scanners, metrology and installed-base services. Its machines pattern the most advanced logic and memory chips on Earth.
companies.sovereignty_role: The single most strategic chokepoint in global semiconductor manufacturing and Europe's crown jewel: no leading-edge chip can be made at scale without ASML's EUV tools.

companies.name: ASM International
companies.description: A leading supplier of wafer-processing equipment, dominant in atomic layer deposition (ALD) used to build gate-all-around and advanced logic transistors. Sells deposition and epitaxy tools to the world's chipmakers.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading deposition-equipment maker and an oligopoly leader in ALD, a process critical to every advanced-node fab.

companies.name: BE Semiconductor (Besi)
companies.description: Maker of semiconductor assembly equipment, especially die-attach and hybrid-bonding tools used in advanced packaging and chiplet integration for AI processors. A near-monopoly in hybrid bonding.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leader in advanced-packaging/hybrid-bonding equipment, an increasingly decisive step for AI-chip performance.

companies.name: AIXTRON
companies.description: Supplier of metal-organic chemical vapour deposition (MOCVD) and other epitaxy systems for compound semiconductors (GaN, SiC, optoelectronics, power and laser devices).
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's compound-semiconductor deposition-equipment champion, enabling GaN/SiC power and photonics manufacturing in Europe.

companies.name: Soitec
companies.description: Maker of engineered substrates (FD-SOI, RF-SOI, Power-SOI, SmartSiC, Photonics-SOI) using its SmartCut layer-transfer technology. Sells wafers to foundries and IDMs; near-monopoly in SOI.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's near-monopoly substrate supplier (RF-SOI for smartphones, FD-SOI for edge AI, Photonics-SOI for AI datacentre optics); upstream enabler of the chip value chain.

companies.name: Siltronic AG
companies.description: One of the world's few makers of hyperpure 200mm/300mm silicon wafers, the base substrate of almost all semiconductors, supplied under long-term contracts from fabs in Germany, the US and Singapore.
companies.sovereignty_role: The principal EU-headquartered silicon-wafer supplier and one of only a handful worldwide, a foundational upstream node for European chip self-sufficiency.

companies.name: Carl Zeiss SMT
companies.description: The ZEISS division that designs and builds the high-performance optics at the heart of ASML's DUV and EUV/High-NA lithography scanners, plus photomask and process-control tools. Sole supplier of EUV projection optics to ASML.
companies.sovereignty_role: An irreplaceable single-source chokepoint inside the only machines that can pattern leading-edge chips; with ASML it gives Europe ownership of the most critical capability in chipmaking.
uncertain: Foundation-owned, not VC-funded; revenue ~€5.06bn & ~9,349 staff are for the full ZEISS SMT segment (FY2024/25); no public valuation.

companies.name: PVA TePla
companies.description: Maker of crystal-growing systems (silicon and SiC) and semiconductor metrology (GHz acoustic microscopy for void/TSV inspection used in HBM and advanced packaging).
companies.sovereignty_role: A niche EU equipment/metrology supplier into the SiC and advanced-packaging supply chains the Chips Act is trying to seed in Europe.

companies.name: LPKF Laser & Electronics
companies.description: Maker of laser-based manufacturing systems; its LIDE process makes glass-core substrates and through-glass vias for advanced AI/HPC chip packaging, alongside PCB-prototyping, welding and solar tools.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European equipment maker positioned for glass-core substrates, an emerging high-value node in AI-chip advanced packaging.

companies.name: STMicroelectronics
companies.description: A broad-line integrated device manufacturer (IDM) in power & analog, microcontrollers (STM32), MEMS/sensors, SiC and RF. Owns fabs in France, Italy, Singapore; FD-SOI and full SiC value chain are distinctive.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of Europe's two flagship chipmakers and the leader in SiC power and FD-SOI, with EU-funded fabs (Crolles, Catania) central to Chips Act capacity.

companies.name: Infineon Technologies
companies.description: Global #1 in power semiconductors and automotive chips, an IDM spanning Si/SiC/GaN power, AURIX auto MCUs, sensors, and connectivity/security (ex-Cypress). Fabs in Dresden, Villach, Kulim.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's largest chipmaker and #1 in power & automotive semis; its EU/state-aid-backed Dresden megafab and AI-datacentre power push are core to EU sovereignty.

companies.name: NXP Semiconductors
companies.description: A leading supplier of microcontrollers, application processors, analog, RF, security and connectivity chips (co-inventor of NFC), focused on automotive, industrial/IoT and comms. Dutch-domiciled in Eindhoven.
companies.sovereignty_role: The largest EU-headquartered chipmaker and dominant European supplier of automotive and secure-edge silicon, though it is listed only on Nasdaq (not an EU exchange).
uncertain: Dutch HQ but US-listed (Nasdaq, USD); no EU listing.

companies.name: X-FAB Silicon Foundries
companies.description: A specialty / analog-mixed-signal foundry on mature nodes (analog, high-voltage BCD, SiC, MEMS, photonics), serving automotive, industrial and medical customers from fabs in France, Germany, the US and Malaysia.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's specialty foundry and a direct IPCEI ME/CT recipient (up to €80m), adding sovereign analog/SiC/MEMS capacity.

companies.name: Nordic Semiconductor
companies.description: A fabless leader in ultra-low-power Bluetooth Low Energy SoCs, also supplying cellular IoT and Wi-Fi connectivity. Its chips power a large share of the world's wireless IoT, wearable and smart-home devices.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's homegrown leader in low-power wireless connectivity silicon, reducing reliance on US/Asian connectivity-chip vendors.

companies.name: Melexis
companies.description: A fabless maker of automotive sensor ICs (magnetic position/current, Hall, time-of-flight) with strong design-in share. Manufactures largely through foundry partner X-FAB.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European automotive-sensor leader, though capital-light and structurally outside direct chip-subsidy flows.

companies.name: IQE plc
companies.description: A leading supplier of compound-semiconductor epitaxial wafers ('epi-wafers') for RF/wireless, photonics (VCSELs/lasers) and advanced sensing, from sites in the UK, US and Taiwan.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's principal independent compound-semiconductor epitaxy supplier, an enabling upstream materials layer for RF and photonics.
uncertain: UK AIM micro-cap, extremely volatile; market cap ~GBP440-540M (~EUR510-625M) on 2026-06-08 — figure approximate. FY2025 ran a strategic review / GBP81m raise that removed bank debt; still listed.

companies.name: Nokia
companies.description: A global supplier of mobile, fixed and IP/optical network infrastructure (RAN, core, transport), with Bell Labs research and a large 5G/6G patent portfolio.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of two European RAN vendors (with Ericsson) underpinning sovereign 5G/6G connectivity and a leader in the EU's SNS-JU 6G programme.

companies.name: Ericsson
companies.description: A global leader in mobile network infrastructure (RAN, core, transport) and enterprise wireless, with a large standards-essential 5G/6G patent portfolio.
companies.sovereignty_role: The other European RAN champion underpinning sovereign 5G/6G; holds an IPCEI-CIS cloud-edge project and an SNS-JU governance seat.

companies.name: Deutsche Telekom
companies.description: Europe's largest telecom operator by revenue (fixed, mobile, broadband; majority owner of T-Mobile US). Its T-Systems arm runs the Open Telekom Cloud and managed/sovereign IT services.
companies.sovereignty_role: Core European connectivity backbone; operates sovereign/managed cloud and is building a ~€1bn NVIDIA-powered 'Industrial AI Cloud' in Munich.

companies.name: Orange SA
companies.description: France's incumbent telecom operator (fixed/mobile across Europe, Africa, Middle East). Orange Business delivers IT/cloud; co-owner of the 'Bleu' sovereign-cloud venture with Capgemini.
companies.sovereignty_role: Core European/African connectivity backbone; co-owns the SecNumCloud-targeted 'Bleu' sovereign cloud and partners with Mistral on sovereign generative AI.

companies.name: iliad SA
companies.description: French telecom group (Free in France, plus Italy and Poland) serving ~52m subscribers, controlled by Xavier Niel who took it private in 2021. Wholly owns cloud provider Scaleway and is investing ~€3bn in cloud & AI.
companies.sovereignty_role: A major independent European operator and, via Scaleway and a €3bn cloud/AI programme, a builder of European-owned AI infrastructure; Niel is a key funder of EU AI (Mistral, Kyutai).

key_investors: Xavier Niel (controlling shareholder; took iliad private 2021 at €182/share)

uncertain: Private since 2021; FY2025 revenue ~€10.35bn used for sizing.

companies.name: SAP
companies.description: The largest European tech company by market cap; global leader in enterprise resource planning (ERP) and business software, transitioning to cloud (S/4HANA, RISE with SAP) with sovereign-cloud offerings.
companies.sovereignty_role: The default application/ERP layer for European enterprise and a direct IPCEI-CIS participant; its sovereign-cloud launches anchor the EU cloud stack.

companies.name: Dassault Systèmes
companies.description: Industrial software leader: the 3DEXPERIENCE platform, CATIA, SOLIDWORKS, DELMIA, SIMULIA and MEDIDATA for PLM, design, simulation and virtual twins across industry and life sciences.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's PLM/CAD/simulation backbone for aerospace, defence, automotive and life-sciences manufacturers, a sovereign alternative to US industrial software.

companies.name: Siemens
companies.description: Industrial technology group spanning automation, industrial software (Siemens Xcelerator/Digital Industries), smart infrastructure, mobility and healthcare. A leader in operational-technology and the industrial metaverse.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's industrial-automation and OT-software anchor; an IPCEI-CIS participant on industrial edge, central to sovereign industrial digitalisation.

companies.name: Amadeus IT Group
companies.description: Global travel-technology provider operating the Amadeus GDS and airline/hospitality IT (Altéa/Navitaire passenger service systems), processing a large share of the world's airline bookings and boardings.
companies.sovereignty_role: European operator of critical travel/transport IT infrastructure underpinning European aviation distribution and operations.

companies.name: Hexagon AB
companies.description: Digital-reality group combining precision metrology and sensors with geospatial and autonomy software across manufacturing, mining, AEC, safety and defence. Reports in EUR despite Stockholm listing.
companies.sovereignty_role: European supplier of metrology, geospatial and reality-capture technology used in defence, manufacturing and critical-infrastructure monitoring.
uncertain: Trades in SEK on Nasdaq Stockholm but reports financials in EUR (revenue/mcap shown in EUR).

companies.name: Capgemini
companies.description: A global IT services and consulting group (digital transformation, cloud, engineering, data/AI). Co-owns the 'Bleu' sovereign-cloud venture with Orange.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's large-scale systems integrator delivering sovereign-cloud and public-sector digital programmes (e.g. Bleu).

companies.name: Sword Group
companies.description: IT services and software-engineering group focused on public sector and EU institutions (plus finance, energy). Builds and maintains sovereign EU-institution systems (eu-LISA border IT, EU Parliament, DG-DIGIT).
companies.sovereignty_role: A profitable EU-institution systems integrator with direct, named EU sovereignty contracts (eu-LISA Entry/Exit System, EU Parliament, FREIA).

companies.name: Software AG
companies.description: Enterprise integration/IoT software firm. Its core webMethods and StreamSets platforms were sold to IBM (€2.13bn, 2024); taken private by Silver Lake and delisted from Frankfurt in February 2024.
companies.sovereignty_role: Historically a flagship German integration vendor; sovereignty relevance diminished after its core was sold to US-based IBM and it was taken private by US PE firm Silver Lake.

key_investors: Silver Lake (via Mosel Bidco; ~93% at 2024 delisting)

uncertain: Delisted Feb 2024; current standalone revenue not publicly reported post-IBM divestiture.

companies.name: OVHcloud
companies.description: Europe's largest cloud-infrastructure provider (IaaS, bare-metal, hosted private cloud) operating its own datacentres and servers. A sovereign-cloud pure-play and GAIA-X founder.
companies.sovereignty_role: The purest listed European sovereign-cloud play; part of the EC's €180m sovereign-cloud framework award and a credible alternative to US hyperscalers.

companies.name: IONOS Group
companies.description: European web-hosting and cloud provider (domains, hosting, IONOS Cloud IaaS), majority-owned by United Internet, serving SMBs and the public sector with ~6.6m customers.
companies.sovereignty_role: A German/European hosting and cloud provider positioned as a sovereign alternative to US hyperscalers for public-sector and SME workloads.

companies.name: Atos / Eviden
companies.description: French IT-services group providing managed services, cloud and cybersecurity. Its Bull/BullSequana HPC, quantum and AI-computing business was sold to the French State (completed Apr 2026); Atos completed a major 2024 debt restructuring and is executing the multi-year 'Genesis' turnaround on a reduced perimeter.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European cybersecurity, managed-services and sovereign-cloud provider important to French/EU digital sovereignty; its supercomputing (Bull) sovereignty role has moved to the now state-owned Bull entity. Remains under financial restructuring.
uncertain: Severe financial distress / 'Genesis' restructuring; Bull/HPC divested to the French State (Apr 2026) so the group no longer owns supercomputing; FY2025 figures preliminary.

companies.name: STACKIT (Schwarz Group)
companies.description: German sovereign cloud provider, the cloud arm of Schwarz Digits (Schwarz Group, owner of Lidl/Kaufland). Operates datacentres in Germany and Austria; expanding into a full 'German hyperscaler' for enterprise and public administration.
companies.sovereignty_role: A flagship fully German-owned sovereign cloud; cooperating with the BSI on public-administration cloud and hosting sovereign SAP/AI workloads (RISE, Aleph Alpha's PhariaAI).
uncertain: No standalone revenue/valuation disclosed (parent Schwarz Digits ~€1.9bn division revenue).

companies.name: Scaleway
companies.description: French public-cloud provider, wholly owned by the iliad Group, offering IaaS, bare-metal and GPU/AI cloud (5,000+ GPUs) across European regions. Customers include Hugging Face.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European-owned public/AI cloud and a pillar of iliad's €3bn cloud & AI programme, supplying GPU capacity to European AI developers.
uncertain: No standalone revenue/valuation disclosed; ~639 employees.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.description: Paris-based developer of open-weight large language models (Mistral/Mixtral) and the Le Chat assistant, offering frontier and small open models, enterprise APIs and on-prem/sovereign deployments.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship sovereign foundation-model champion and the principal European alternative to US frontier-AI labs; central to the EU's AI sovereignty agenda.

companies.name: Aleph Alpha
companies.description: Heidelberg-based enterprise/sovereign AI company building explainable LLMs (Luminous/Pharia) and the PhariaAI platform for governments and regulated industries, with on-prem data-sovereign deployments.
companies.sovereignty_role: Germany's headline sovereign-AI company, targeting public sector and defence; backed by the Schwarz Group as a strategic sovereignty play.
uncertain: Standalone valuation effectively undisclosed; pending Cohere merger (announced Apr 2026, not closed).

companies.name: DeepL
companies.description: Cologne-based neural machine-translation company (DeepL Translator, Write, Voice) across 30+ languages, widely regarded as best-in-class on translation quality.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European language-AI leader and credible homegrown alternative to US translation big-tech for GDPR-compliant, EU-based language processing.
uncertain: Latest priced round May 2024; may be stale.

companies.name: Helsing
companies.description: Munich-based defence-AI company building AI software and autonomous systems (strike drones, sensor-fusion for Eurofighter/Gripen) for air, land, sea and sub-surface domains; supplies systems to Ukraine.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's pre-eminent sovereign defence-AI company, explicitly framed around European technological sovereignty, with large European government drone/defence contracts.
uncertain: Larger ~$18bn round reported May 2026 but not yet closed; June-2025 figures used.

companies.name: Thales Group
companies.description: Defence electronics, secure communications, encryption and digital identity/cybersecurity, avionics and space. Parent of Thales Alenia Space (67%) and Thales SIX. Partly French-state owned.
companies.sovereignty_role: Anchor of European sovereign defence electronics, secure/encrypted comms and digital identity; key IRIS² subcontractor and supplier of sovereign cryptography.

companies.name: Leonardo S.p.A.
companies.description: Italian defence and aerospace group: electronics for land/sea/air, helicopters, cyber and security. Owns 33% of Thales Alenia Space; Italian state (MEF) is the largest shareholder.
companies.sovereignty_role: State-backed pillar of European defence/aerospace electronics and cyber; co-owner of Thales Alenia Space and a player in EU defence consolidation.

companies.name: Rheinmetall AG
companies.description: Germany's largest defence company: land-defence systems, munitions, military vehicles and weapon systems, plus defence electronics, sensors and air defence. Rapidly expanding on European rearmament.
companies.sovereignty_role: Central supplier to the Bundeswehr and European armed forces and a flagship of Europe's rearmament and defence-industrial sovereignty.

companies.name: Saab AB
companies.description: Swedish defence and aerospace group: Gripen fighter, sensors/radar, electronic warfare (Arexis), naval (Kockums), surveillance and missiles. Strong European order growth.
companies.sovereignty_role: Sweden's sovereign defence-aerospace prime supplying fighters, sensors, EW and submarines to Europe/NATO; strategic partner and investor in Helsing.

companies.name: WALLIX Group
companies.description: European cybersecurity vendor specialising in Privileged Access Management (PAM) and identity, unified under the WALLIX One platform. Dual BSI (Germany) + ANSSI (France) certified.
companies.sovereignty_role: The purest listed European sovereign-cyber pure-play (identity/access), positioned for NIS2/DORA-driven sovereign demand.

companies.name: WithSecure
companies.description: Enterprise cybersecurity vendor (WithSecure Elements: EPP/EDR/XDR, exposure management) spun out of F-Secure in 2022. Taken private by a CVC- and Risto Siilasmaa-led consortium and delisted from Nasdaq Helsinki in March 2026.
companies.sovereignty_role: A Finnish/European enterprise-cybersecurity provider offering a sovereign alternative to US endpoint/cloud-security vendors; now privately held.
uncertain: Delisted 24 Mar 2026 (taken private at €1.70/share, ~€292m); FY2024 revenue €116m continuing ops.

companies.name: IQM Quantum Computers
companies.description: Espoo-based builder of full-stack superconducting quantum computers (IQM Radiance/Spark), selling on-prem systems and integrating them into HPC centres. 20+ systems sold by end-2025.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading superconducting quantum-hardware company, supplying on-soil quantum machines to EuroHPC centres (LRZ, VTT/CSC).
uncertain: SPAC merger (RAAQ) board-approved, expected ~June 2026, not yet closed; valuation implied by deal.

companies.name: Pasqal
companies.description: Palaiseau-based neutral-atom quantum computing company building analog/digital processors (100-140+ qubits) and delivering them to HPC centres.
companies.sovereignty_role: A leading European neutral-atom quantum-hardware champion supplying EU sovereign HPC-quantum infrastructure (Jülich, GENCI-CEA, CINECA) under France 2030.
uncertain: Valuation ~EUR1.73bn is implied by the Bleichroeder (BBCQ) SPAC signed 4 Mar 2026 (not yet closed); >EUR300m total funding to date; remains private as of 2026-06-08.

companies.name: Quandela
companies.description: Massy-based photonic quantum computing company building single-photon-source-based machines (Belenos/Canopus), spun out of CNRS/C2N.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading photonic quantum-computing company, delivering on-soil machines to EuroHPC/CEA and offering QPUs on OVHcloud's sovereign cloud.
uncertain: No public post-money valuation disclosed.

companies.name: Airbus SE
companies.description: European aerospace and defence prime. Airbus Defence and Space builds Earth-observation, telecom and navigation satellites, is a core IRIS² partner, and (with ArianeGroup) anchors European launchers; also a major military-aircraft prime.
companies.sovereignty_role: Anchor European space and defence prime: sovereign satellite manufacturing, launcher industrial base, and IRIS² subcontractor for EU secure connectivity.

companies.name: Thales Alenia Space
companies.description: Satellite-systems manufacturer (telecom, Earth observation, navigation, exploration, orbital infrastructure), a 67% Thales / 33% Leonardo joint venture. Core industrial subcontractor on IRIS².
companies.sovereignty_role: A major European sovereign satellite prime for telecom, observation, navigation and secure connectivity (IRIS²); Franco-Italian pillar of European space autonomy.
uncertain: JV; standalone valuation n/a; revenue ~€2.23bn (2024).

companies.name: OHB SE
companies.description: German space and technology group (Bremen): satellite manufacturing and space systems; prime contractor for Galileo navigation satellites and an IRIS² subcontractor. Remains listed (low free float) after KKR's 2023 minority investment.
companies.sovereignty_role: Germany's leading independent space-systems prime — Galileo prime contractor and IRIS² subcontractor — central to European navigation and secure-connectivity sovereignty.
uncertain: KKR (~29%) is exiting, placing ~20pp by end-June 2026 to lift free float from ~6% toward ~26%; Fuchs family remains anchor. P/E distorted during the placement.

companies.name: Eutelsat Group
companies.description: The first integrated GEO-LEO satellite operator, formed by the 2023 Eutelsat-OneWeb merger (~35 GEO satellites plus the OneWeb LEO constellation). Core member of the SpaceRISE consortium delivering IRIS². French State is largest shareholder (~30%).
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's sovereign GEO+LEO connectivity operator; OneWeb LEO underpins secure/government and Ukraine connectivity, and Eutelsat is a core IRIS² operator.
uncertain: FY ends 30 June; French State became largest shareholder (~29.65%) via 2025 capital raise.

companies.name: SES S.A.
companies.description: One of the world's largest satellite operators, running a combined GEO + MEO fleet (incl. the O3b mPOWER constellation); consolidated Intelsat in 2025. Serves government/defence, telco, aviation, maritime and media.
companies.sovereignty_role: An EU-listed sovereign satellite-communications backbone supplying secure government/NATO connectivity (e.g. NSPA) independent of US/Chinese operators.
uncertain: SES reports in EUR; FY2025 revenue EUR2,627m (Intelsat consolidated from Jul 2025); loss-making post-integration; dual-listed Paris/Luxembourg.

companies.name: Hensoldt AG
companies.description: German defence-electronics champion in radar, optronics, electronic warfare and sensors (air-defence radars, Eurofighter, PEGASUS). Record FY2025 order intake EUR4.71bn, backlog ~EUR8.8bn.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading independent sensor-and-radar house for sovereign air defence, ISR and electronic warfare; core beneficiary of EU/NATO rearmament.

companies.name: Indra Sistemas S.A.
companies.description: Spain's leading defence, aerospace and digital-technology group (radar/EW, air-traffic management, secure comms, IT). In Mar 2026 acquired ~89.7% of satellite operator Hispasat for EUR725m.
companies.sovereignty_role: A consolidating Spanish/European defence-tech and space prime anchoring Iberian sovereign radar, ATM, secure comms and satellite capability.

companies.name: Kongsberg Gruppen ASA
companies.description: Norwegian defence and maritime-technology group; ~80% defence/aerospace incl. the Naval/Joint Strike Missile, remote weapon stations and command systems, plus maritime and digital divisions.
companies.sovereignty_role: A top-tier European (EEA) missile and defence-systems supplier central to NATO/European deterrence and sovereign munitions/naval capability.
uncertain: Norway is EEA/NATO, not EU. Reports in NOK (converted to EUR).

companies.name: secunet Security Networks AG
companies.description: Germany's leading IT-security company and the Federal Republic's primary IT-security partner (SINA secure networks) for public administration, defence, healthcare and police. Majority-owned by Giesecke+Devrient.
companies.sovereignty_role: The trusted supplier of high-assurance, nationally certified cybersecurity for German/EU government and critical infrastructure - a direct embodiment of sovereign cybersecurity.

companies.name: Dassault Aviation S.A.
companies.description: French aerospace prime building the Rafale combat aircraft and Falcon business jets. Controlled by Groupe Industriel Marcel Dassault (~66.9%); Airbus holds ~10.7%. Distinct from Dassault Systemes (software).
companies.sovereignty_role: Maker of Europe's sovereign Rafale fighter and a pillar of the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) - core to European autonomy in combat aviation.
uncertain: Founding year ~1929 (Avions Marcel Bloch); wider Dassault origins cited as 1916.

companies.name: RENK Group AG
companies.description: Augsburg maker of transmissions and drive systems for tracked military vehicles (e.g. Leopard), plus naval and industrial gearboxes. IPO'd Feb 2024.
companies.sovereignty_role: A near-irreplaceable supplier of armoured-vehicle transmissions to European land-systems primes - a critical-component chokepoint in the sovereign tank supply chain.

companies.name: KNDS N.V.
companies.description: Franco-German land-systems champion (2015 merger of Krauss-Maffei Wegmann and Nexter); makes the Leopard 2 tank and CAESAR howitzer. FY2025 revenue ~EUR4.4bn (+16%), backlog ~EUR33bn. A Frankfurt-Paris IPO is targeted for ~mid-2026.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship sovereign tank-and-artillery maker and backbone of the Main Ground Combat System (MGCS) - central to European land-defence autonomy.
uncertain: Private as of Jun 2026; Frankfurt/Paris IPO planned summer 2026 - prospective ~EUR20bn valuation, not a closed round.

companies.name: AT&S
companies.description: Leading European maker of high-end printed circuit boards and IC substrates for semiconductors (mobile, automotive, AI/HPC), with plants in Austria, China, India and Korea.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's principal IC-substrate / advanced-PCB maker - the chip-to-system packaging layer that Chips Act 2.0 flags as a European supply-chain gap.
uncertain: Much substrate capacity is China-located; sovereignty contribution is HQ/IP plus Austrian production.

companies.name: SUSS MicroTec SE
companies.description: Garching-based semiconductor-equipment maker: photolithography (coater/developer), bonding and photomask-cleaning systems for chip, MEMS and advanced-packaging manufacturing.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European front-end/advanced-packaging equipment supplier strengthening the EU fab-equipment base alongside ASML/Aixtron under Chips Act 2.0.

companies.name: Telefonica S.A.
companies.description: Spain's incumbent telecom operator and one of Europe's largest (fixed, mobile, fibre, B2B/cloud-security) across Europe and Latin America. The Spanish state (SEPI) is a major shareholder.
companies.sovereignty_role: A strategically protected European telecom incumbent and critical-infrastructure operator; its fibre/5G networks underpin sovereign connectivity.

companies.name: Telecom Italia (TIM)
companies.description: Italy's incumbent telecom operator (fixed, mobile, enterprise/cloud). Subject to Italian state 'golden power' oversight and state involvement in its strategic fibre backbone (NetCo/FiberCop).
companies.sovereignty_role: Operator of Italy's critical fixed/mobile networks and strategic fibre - a core sovereign-connectivity asset under the package's digital-networks priority.
uncertain: Ordinary shares TIT.MI (savings line TITR.MI excluded).

companies.name: Koninklijke KPN N.V.
companies.description: The Netherlands' incumbent telecom operator (fixed, mobile, fibre broadband, plus cloud and security services to consumers, business and wholesale).
companies.sovereignty_role: Operator of the Netherlands' critical fibre/mobile infrastructure and national cloud/security services - a sovereign-connectivity backbone for a country hosting ASML and major datacentre capacity.

companies.name: Bechtle AG
companies.description: Germany's largest IT system house and a leading European IT integrator: IT infrastructure, multi-cloud and managed services, IT security and e-commerce. ~16,000 employees.
companies.sovereignty_role: A major channel for deploying sovereign cloud, open-source and security solutions to European public administrations and enterprises.

companies.name: Reply S.p.A.
companies.description: Italian IT consulting / system-integration / digital-services network with strong AI, cloud, cybersecurity and Industry-4.0 practices.
companies.sovereignty_role: A leading European digital-transformation and AI/cloud integrator delivering sovereign-relevant solutions to European enterprises and the public sector.

companies.name: Black Forest Labs
companies.description: Freiburg-based generative-AI lab and Europe's leading image/visual foundation-model company (the FLUX models), founded by former Stability AI researchers behind Stable Diffusion.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship open/visual foundation-model developer - a sovereign counterweight in generative-media AI alongside Mistral on language.
uncertain: USD-reported ($300M Series B at $3.25bn, Dec 2025); EUR approx.

companies.name: H Company
companies.description: Paris AI lab (formerly Holistic AI) building agentic AI models that automate business processes; raised a ~$220M seed in 2024, among Europe's largest.
companies.sovereignty_role: A French sovereign frontier-AI lab focused on autonomous agents, backed by leading European industrialists; part of France's bid for independent AI capability.
uncertain: ~$220M seed (USD), EUR approx; no public valuation; cap table changed after 2024 co-founder departures.

companies.name: Alice & Bob
companies.description: Paris quantum-computing company pursuing fault-tolerant machines based on self-correcting cat qubits; building a EUR50M cat-qubit fab in Paris.
companies.sovereignty_role: A French sovereign quantum-hardware champion selected for France's EUR500M PROQCIMA fault-tolerant-quantum programme.
uncertain: EUR100M Series B (Jan 2025) + undisclosed NVentures extension; no public valuation.

companies.name: planqc GmbH
companies.description: Garching (Munich) quantum company building neutral-atom quantum processors; leading a government-funded project to deliver a 1,000-qubit machine to the Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ).
companies.sovereignty_role: Germany's leading neutral-atom quantum-hardware company, embedded in Munich Quantum Valley, supplying sovereign quantum compute to a national HPC centre.
uncertain: EUR50M Series A (2024); public projects (LRZ/DLR) separate from equity; no public valuation.

companies.name: ICEYE
companies.description: Finnish operator of the world's largest synthetic-aperture-radar (SAR) satellite constellation, providing all-weather Earth observation for defence, intelligence, disaster and maritime monitoring. 2025 revenue >EUR250m.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading commercial SAR/ISR provider, increasingly supplying sovereign defence and intelligence imagery to European governments.
uncertain: ~$2.8bn valuation (Dec 2025), EUR approx; a larger round (up to ~EUR5bn) reported in talks, not confirmed.

companies.name: Isar Aerospace SE
companies.description: Munich-area launch-vehicle company developing the Spectrum small/medium orbital rocket; the most capitalised independent New Space company in Europe (>EUR400m raised), with ESA/EC launch agreements.
companies.sovereignty_role: A frontrunner for European sovereign access to space via commercial launch, reducing dependence on non-European launchers.
uncertain: >EUR400m total incl. a EUR150m 2025 convertible; no public valuation; ESA/EC support separate from equity.

companies.name: The Exploration Company
companies.description: Franco-German space company (Munich/Bordeaux) developing 'Nyx', a reusable orbital cargo (later crew) capsule incl. space-station resupply; flew a demonstrator in 2025.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading effort toward a sovereign orbital cargo/crew capsule - a key gap in European in-space transport autonomy.
uncertain: ~$160M Series B (2024), EUR approx; no public valuation.

companies.name: D-Orbit S.p.A.
companies.description: Italian space-logistics company operating the ION orbital transfer vehicle (satellite deployment, hosted payloads), expanding into in-space computing. Series D led by Azimut from Jan 2026.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading in-space logistics/orbital-services provider - a building block for sovereign, flexible European operations in orbit.
uncertain: Total funding ~EUR250m approx; no public post-money valuation.

companies.name: Jenoptik AG
companies.description: German photonics group; its Advanced Photonic Solutions arm supplies precision optics, lasers and metrology used in semiconductor lithography and wafer inspection.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European supplier of precision optics/photonic subsystems into the semiconductor capital-equipment chain, reducing reliance on US/Japanese optics.
uncertain: Semiconductor optics is one segment of a broader group (also mobility/metrology).

companies.name: Elmos Semiconductor SE
companies.description: Dortmund-based designer of automotive mixed-signal semiconductors (sensor ICs, motor/LED drivers, system ICs) for airbags, lighting, access and comfort electronics.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of the few European-owned automotive-semiconductor specialists, supporting EU auto chip supply security alongside Infineon, NXP, STMicro and Melexis.

companies.name: ams-OSRAM AG
companies.description: Austrian-headquartered optical-semiconductor and sensor group (LEDs, VCSEL lasers, photonic and CMOS sensor ICs) for automotive, consumer, industrial and medical markets; formed from the 2020 ams/OSRAM merger.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship optical-semiconductor and sensing player (automotive lighting, 3D sensing, photonics), reducing reliance on Asian/US emitter and sensor suppliers.
uncertain: Austria-HQ (EU) but PRIMARY LISTING is SIX Swiss in CHF; market cap converted from CHF. Loss-making FY2025 (net loss ~EUR188m) so P/E n/a.

companies.name: SiPearl
companies.description: French fabless designer of energy-efficient HPC microprocessors; its Arm-based Rhea CPU family powers the European Processor Initiative and Europe's exascale supercomputers.
companies.sovereignty_role: Cornerstone of the EU's effort to build a sovereign high-performance-compute processor, reducing dependence on US x86/GPU vendors for supercomputing and AI.
uncertain: Private; no public valuation — total funding (~EUR130m) used as size proxy.

companies.name: SMART Photonics
companies.description: Eindhoven-based independent pure-play foundry for indium-phosphide (InP) photonic integrated circuits; a central node in the Dutch/European integrated-photonics ecosystem (PhotonDelta).
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's leading open-access InP photonic-chip foundry, underpinning sovereign integrated-photonics capability for telecom, sensing and AI interconnect.
uncertain: Private; no public valuation — total funding (~EUR220m) used as size proxy.

companies.name: Telia Company AB
companies.description: The Nordic/Baltic incumbent telecom operator (fixed, mobile, broadband, B2B/cloud, TV) across Sweden, Finland, Norway, Denmark and the Baltics. Swedish state is the largest shareholder.
companies.sovereignty_role: Operator of critical fixed/mobile/subsea infrastructure across the Nordics and Baltics; a state-influenced incumbent central to regional sovereign connectivity on NATO's eastern flank.
uncertain: mcap/revenue converted from SEK at EUR=SEK10.90.

companies.name: Telenor ASA
companies.description: Norway's incumbent telecom operator (mobile, fixed, broadband) with Nordic and Asian operations and a towers/infrastructure arm. Norwegian state holds a controlling stake.
companies.sovereignty_role: Operator of Norway's critical mobile/fixed and subsea-cable infrastructure; majority state-owned EEA incumbent underpinning sovereign Nordic connectivity for a NATO member.
uncertain: Norway = EEA, not EU (consistent with Kongsberg/Nordic Semi). Reports in NOK.

companies.name: Proximus SA
companies.description: Belgium's incumbent telecom operator (fixed, mobile, fibre, ICT/cloud via Proximus NXT, plus BICS carrier and Telesign digital-identity). Belgian federal state is majority shareholder.
companies.sovereignty_role: Operator of Belgium's critical networks serving the EU-institutions home country; majority state-owned sovereign-connectivity backbone for Brussels-hosted EU bodies.

companies.name: Tele2 AB
companies.description: Swedish/Baltic telecom operator (mobile, fixed broadband, TV, IoT) operating mainly in Sweden, the Baltics and via a global IoT business.
companies.sovereignty_role: A challenger operator of critical mobile/broadband and IoT connectivity in Sweden and the Baltics, adding resilience to Nordic-Baltic sovereign networks.
uncertain: Converted from SEK at EUR=SEK10.90; employees approximate.

companies.name: Cellnex Telecom SA
companies.description: Europe's largest independent wireless telecom-tower operator, with 100k+ sites across 11 countries leased to mobile operators (towers, small cells, broadcast, fibre backhaul).
companies.sovereignty_role: The leading neutral-host European mobile-infrastructure owner; operator-independent passive infrastructure underpinning multi-operator 5G and sovereign connectivity.
uncertain: Tower-REIT model: net loss FY2025 (P/E n/a) and high net debt typical of the sector.

companies.name: CANCOM SE
companies.description: German IT-services and hybrid/multi-cloud provider running a Cloud Marketplace that integrates sovereign European clouds (STACKIT, IONOS) alongside hyperscalers, plus managed services and cybersecurity.
companies.sovereignty_role: A German integrator channelling public-sector and regulated workloads onto sovereign European clouds; a distribution layer for EU Cloud & AI Development Act-compliant infrastructure.

companies.name: Northern Data AG
companies.description: German HPC/AI-infrastructure group operating Taiga Cloud (NVIDIA H100/H200 GPU cloud, ~24k GPUs) and Ardent data centres; an NVIDIA-designated sovereign-AI cloud provider.
companies.sovereignty_role: One of Europe's largest sovereign AI-cloud / GPU-infrastructure operators, relevant to the EU Cloud & AI Development Act's compute-sovereignty goals.
uncertain: Distressed/speculative: FY2025 revenue only ~EUR80m (-34%), large net loss; revenue lags the deployed-capacity narrative.

companies.name: LightOn
companies.description: Paris-based enterprise generative-AI company; its Paradigm platform delivers on-prem/sovereign GenAI (LLMs, RAG, agents) to corporates and the public sector. First pure-play GenAI company listed in Europe (IPO Nov 2024).
companies.sovereignty_role: The only listed European pure-play sovereign generative-AI vendor, targeting GDPR-compliant on-prem deployments for French/EU government and regulated enterprises.
uncertain: Micro-cap (~EUR24m), down heavily from IPO; only market cap verified, other financials left null.

companies.name: Kyutai
companies.description: Paris-based NON-PROFIT open-science AI research lab (behind the Moshi speech model); releases open models, code and papers. Funded by a ~EUR300m 5-year commitment from Iliad/Xavier Niel, CMA CGM and Schmidt Sciences.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship open-science AI lab and a sovereign open-research counterweight to closed US labs; positioned alongside (and distinct from) Mistral as fully open.
uncertain: NON-PROFIT lab, not a company — no valuation/cap table; the EUR300m is a 5-year funding commitment used for sizing only.

companies.name: eleQtron
companies.description: Siegen/Hamburg-based trapped-ion quantum-computing scale-up (University of Siegen spin-off) using proprietary MAGIC microwave control; >EUR60m order backlog. Closed a EUR57m Series A in May 2026.
companies.sovereignty_role: A German sovereign trapped-ion quantum-hardware champion (Schwarz Digits / EIC-backed) supplying European research and HPC centres.
uncertain: No public valuation; EUR57m is the Series A round size.

companies.name: AQT (Alpine Quantum Technologies)
companies.description: Innsbruck-based trapped-ion quantum-computing company (University of Innsbruck spin-off) building rack-mounted, room-temperature ion-trap systems sold to EU HPC centres.
companies.sovereignty_role: Austria's leading sovereign trapped-ion quantum-hardware maker, delivering on-soil rack-format quantum machines to EuroHPC/research centres.
uncertain: Grant- and contract-funded (no disclosed VC round/valuation); size_eur left null.

companies.name: ParityQC
companies.description: Innsbruck-based quantum-architecture company providing hardware-agnostic 'ParityQC Architecture' blueprints and the ParityOS operating system for scalable, error-corrected quantum computers.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European sovereign quantum-architecture/software-IP provider enabling EU-built quantum machines across hardware platforms.
uncertain: Asset-light (architecture/OS); grant-funded, no disclosed equity round/valuation; size_eur null.

companies.name: Kipu Quantum
companies.description: Karlsruhe/Berlin-based quantum-software company building application- and hardware-specific compressed quantum algorithms (hardware-agnostic).
companies.sovereignty_role: A German sovereign quantum-algorithm/software layer maximising near-term value from EU quantum hardware across providers.
uncertain: ~EUR13m total funding; no public valuation.

companies.name: QuiX Quantum
companies.description: Enschede-based photonic quantum-computing company (University of Twente roots) building room-temperature universal photonic quantum computers; sold systems to Germany's DLR QCI.
companies.sovereignty_role: The Netherlands' leading sovereign photonic quantum-hardware company, supplying on-soil systems to EU research/aerospace (DLR) and EuroHPC.
uncertain: EUR15m Series A; no public valuation.

companies.name: Safran SA
companies.description: Aerospace & defence group: aircraft engines (CFM/LEAP JV with GE) plus Safran Electronics & Defense (optronics, inertial navigation, sights, drones, avionics).
companies.sovereignty_role: European sovereign supplier of military propulsion, inertial navigation and optronics (independent of US GPS/optics) and a pillar of Franco-European combat-air and missile supply chains.

companies.name: Sopra Steria
companies.description: Major French IT-services / systems-integration group with a software arm (Sopra Banking Software). Significant defence, security and government / sovereign-IT business across the EU.
companies.sovereignty_role: Large European sovereign-IT and cyber systems integrator for defence ministries, EU institutions and critical infrastructure.
uncertain: Spans defence/cyber and general IT; classified under Enterprise software (like Capgemini).

companies.name: Czechoslovak Group (CSG)
companies.description: Vertically integrated European defence and ammunition group (management HQ Prague, Dutch holding): Europe's #1 small-calibre and a top medium/large-calibre ammunition producer, plus military vehicles. IPO'd Jan 2026.
companies.sovereignty_role: Central supplier of artillery shells/ammunition to Europe and Ukraine and a key beneficiary of the EU shell-production ramp; a sovereign land-munitions champion in central Europe.
uncertain: IPO'd 23 Jan 2026; market cap volatile (sources span EUR14.9-18.7bn by share-count basis) - ~EUR15bn used.

companies.name: Exosens SA
companies.description: Electro-optical technology group (Photonis, Telops, Xenics): image-intensifier tubes for night vision, plus infrared/thermal cameras and detectors. Defence & surveillance ~75% of revenue.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's sovereign maker of night-vision image-intensifier tubes (a critical, export-controlled component) - the key non-US/non-Chinese supplier to European night-vision and ISR programmes.
uncertain: Founded year reflects Photonis heritage (1937); listed since 2024 IPO.

companies.name: Theon International Plc
companies.description: Manufacturer of night-vision goggles, thermal imaging and electro-optical ISR systems for European/NATO armed forces; production in Greece/Germany/Baltics. Holds a ~9.8% stake in Exosens.
companies.sovereignty_role: Leading European sovereign supplier of soldier night-vision/optronics, a core dismounted-soldier capability for EU/NATO procurement.
uncertain: Employee count (618) is end-2024; likely higher now.

companies.name: Exail Technologies SA
companies.description: Robotics, maritime and navigation-technology group (ECA Group / iXblue lineage): autonomous naval drones (USVs/AUVs), inertial navigation systems, sonars and photonics.
companies.sovereignty_role: European sovereign provider of inertial navigation (GPS-independent) and naval autonomous systems / mine-countermeasure drones for European navies.
uncertain: 'Founded 2022' = Exail brand from iXblue/ECA merger; corporate lineage older.

companies.name: Rohde & Schwarz
companies.description: Privately held Munich technology group: test & measurement, secure communications and cryptography, radiomonitoring/EW, and network/IT cybersecurity. One of Europe's largest private defence-electronics firms.
companies.sovereignty_role: German sovereign supplier of secure/encrypted government and military communications, crypto and signals intelligence - a privately controlled European alternative.

key_investors: Rohde and Schwarz founding families (privately held)

uncertain: Private (family-owned); no equity valuation. Net revenue ~EUR3.16bn FY2024/25 used for sizing; >15,000 employees.

companies.name: Diehl Stiftung & Co. KG
companies.description: Family-owned German industrial group; Diehl Defence makes the IRIS-T air-to-air and IRIS-T SLM/SLS ground-based air-defence missile systems and guided munitions (group also spans metering, aviation, controls).
companies.sovereignty_role: Germany's sovereign air-defence-missile maker (IRIS-T), central to European ground-based air defence (incl. European Sky Shield) and supplies to Ukraine.
uncertain: Private (family/foundation). Group FY2024 sales ~EUR4.70bn used for sizing; Diehl Defence segment ~EUR1.83bn.

companies.name: Patria Group
companies.description: Finnish defence group: armoured vehicles (Patria 6x6/AMV), mortar systems, life-cycle support and (via Nammo, 50%) ammunition. Owned by the State of Finland (50.1%) and Norway's Kongsberg (49.9%).
companies.sovereignty_role: Nordic sovereign armoured-vehicle and systems supplier; anchor of the multinational Patria 6x6 / CAVS programme across Finland, Sweden, Latvia and Germany.
uncertain: State/Kongsberg-owned, not listed. FY2025 net sales ~EUR1.09bn used for sizing; also holds 50% of Nammo.

companies.name: MBDA
companies.description: Pan-European guided-missile champion (Aster air-defence interceptors, SCALP/Storm Shadow cruise missiles, Exocet, Mistral, anti-tank). JV of Airbus (37.5%), BAE Systems (37.5%) and Leonardo (25%).
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's primary sovereign complex-weapons / missile house and the backbone of European air-defence and deep-strike munition supply.
uncertain: Private JV; one owner (BAE, 37.5%) is UK. FY2024 revenue ~EUR4.9bn used for sizing.

companies.name: HarfangLab
companies.description: French endpoint-detection-and-response (EDR) vendor founded by ex-French-military/ANSSI cyber experts; first ANSSI-CSPN-certified EDR and ANSSI-qualified (2024).
companies.sovereignty_role: The leading ANSSI-qualified sovereign EDR - a European alternative to US endpoint security for state and critical-infrastructure use under SecNumCloud/NIS2.
uncertain: Private; EUR25m Series A (2023); revenue not public.

companies.name: Sekoia.io
companies.description: French SaaS SOC / Open-XDR and cyber-threat-intelligence platform; clients include EDF, Vinci, SNCF and the French Ministry of Armed Forces.
companies.sovereignty_role: Sovereign European SOC/XDR and CTI platform - a French alternative to US SIEM/XDR clouds, used by French defence and critical infrastructure.
uncertain: Private; EUR60m total raised; ~100-150 employees.

companies.name: TEHTRIS
companies.description: French XDR vendor focused on automated neutralisation of cyberattacks; the TEHTRIS XDR Platform operates in 120+ countries. Founded by former French intelligence/military cyber specialists.
companies.sovereignty_role: Sovereign French XDR/EDR pure-play marketed as a European alternative to US detection-and-response platforms.
uncertain: Private; ~EUR64m total raised; ~200-260 employees.

companies.name: Nemetschek
companies.description: European leader in Architecture, Engineering, Construction & Operations (AEC/O) software (Allplan, Graphisoft/Archicad, Bluebeam) plus media/entertainment (Maxon). Crossed EUR1bn revenue in FY2025; ~92% recurring.
companies.sovereignty_role: Europe's flagship BIM/construction-software vendor - a sovereign alternative to US Autodesk for the digitalisation of EU construction and infrastructure.
uncertain: FY2025 growth partly acquisition-driven.

companies.name: TeamViewer
companies.description: Remote-connectivity and digital-workplace software (remote access/support, plus digital employee experience after the 2024 acquisition of 1E). FY2025 IFRS revenue ~EUR747m, Adj. EBITDA margin ~44%.
companies.sovereignty_role: A scaled German SaaS vendor for secure remote access/IT management - a European option versus US incumbents for enterprise and industrial remote operations.
uncertain: Carries net debt from the ~USD720m 1E acquisition; stock down sharply YoY.

companies.name: Tieto (Tietoevry)
companies.description: Nordic software & digital-engineering group (banking, public-sector/care, industry verticals + consulting). Divested Tech Services in 2025 and rebranded to 'Tieto'. FY2025 continuing-ops revenue ~EUR1.85bn.
companies.sovereignty_role: A core Nordic IT/software supplier to banks and government; key digital backbone for Finnish/Nordic public administration.
uncertain: Revenue is continuing-ops only post Tech Services divestiture; Yahoo ticker still TIETO.HE.

companies.name: Nagarro
companies.description: Munich-HQ digital-engineering and custom-software services firm (~18,000 staff, 38 countries). FY2025 audited revenue ~EUR1.0bn.
companies.sovereignty_role: EU-domiciled digital product-engineering provider for European enterprise software/digitalisation needs.
uncertain: Governance flag: an independent investigation concluded alongside FY2025 results (audited statements reaffirmed figures).

companies.name: Wolters Kluwer
companies.description: Global professional-information, software & services group (health; tax & accounting; financial & corporate compliance; legal & regulatory). FY2025 revenue ~EUR6.1bn; majority recurring software/digital.
companies.sovereignty_role: Large EU-domiciled provider of regulatory/compliance and expert software embedded in European legal, tax, healthcare and financial workflows.
uncertain: Information-services + software (not pure software); market cap converted from USD, approximate.

companies.name: n8n
companies.description: Berlin-based fair-code workflow-automation platform and a leading European AI-agent / orchestration layer; embedded into SAP's Joule Studio. ~EUR4.5bn valuation after a May 2026 SAP investment.
companies.sovereignty_role: A European AI-automation/agent-orchestration champion, strategically tied to SAP as the agentic layer for sovereign EU enterprise software stacks.
uncertain: $5.2bn (~EUR4.49bn) valuation after SAP's May-2026 investment; AI-agent orchestration (classified as enterprise software).

companies.name: Avio
companies.description: Prime contractor for the Vega/Vega-C small launchers and maker of the P120C/P160C solid boosters used on both Vega and Ariane 6; also solid-propulsion for defence. FY2025 revenue ~EUR542m.
companies.sovereignty_role: Italy's launch champion and a pillar of Europe's autonomous access to space; sole maker of Vega and key Ariane-6 boosters, central to ESA/EU sovereign launch capability.
uncertain: Market cap converted from USD ~$2.0bn, approximate. Listed on Euronext Milan STAR segment.

companies.name: GomSpace
companies.description: Danish (Aalborg) maker of nanosatellites, components and turnkey smallsat solutions for defence, institutional and commercial customers. FY2025 revenue ~SEK442m (+72% YoY).
companies.sovereignty_role: European nanosatellite/SmallSat specialist supplying defence and institutional constellations - a sovereign-EU option in the smallsat platform/component layer.
uncertain: Reports in SEK (converted at EUR=SEK10.90); listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market.

companies.name: Hisdesat
companies.description: Spanish government satellite-services operator for secure/military communications (Spainsat NG) and Earth observation (PAZ SAR). Shareholders incl. Hispasat, ISDEFE (state), Airbus D&S, Indra, SENER.
companies.sovereignty_role: Operator of Spain's sovereign milcomms and SAR reconnaissance satellites for the Armed Forces - core Spanish/EU defence space infrastructure.
uncertain: Government/consortium owned (not a startup); no market cap/valuation - size_eur null. Indra's Hispasat acquisition is shifting effective control.

companies.name: Telespazio
companies.description: Leonardo/Thales 'Space Alliance' JV for satellite services: ground segment, satellite operations, geo-information, SATCOM and Galileo/PNT services. Sister company to Thales Alenia Space.
companies.sovereignty_role: Operates ground infrastructure and downstream services underpinning Galileo, Copernicus and government SATCOM - a backbone of EU sovereign space services.
uncertain: Private JV; revenue ~EUR1bn used as a size proxy (approx). Pending Airbus+Leonardo+Thales space-merger MoU (Oct 2025) may change structure.

companies.name: Rocket Factory Augsburg (RFA)
companies.description: German micro-launcher developer (RFA ONE) majority-owned by OHB; targeting low-cost orbital launch, with a first launch attempt slated for 2026 after a 2024 stage-test anomaly.
companies.sovereignty_role: A contender in Europe's sovereign small-launch race (ESA/EU launcher initiatives), reducing dependence on foreign launch providers.
uncertain: OHB-controlled subsidiary; ~EUR240m valuation; first orbital launch repeatedly slipped to 2026.

companies.name: Latitude
companies.description: French (Reims) micro-launcher startup developing the Zephyr rocket (~100 kg to LEO); backed by Bpifrance/France 2030.
companies.sovereignty_role: French entrant in the European sovereign small-launch race.
uncertain: ~EUR47m equity raised (excl. EUR15m France-2030 grant); no public valuation.

companies.name: Hemeria
companies.description: French (Toulouse) nanosatellite & microsatellite manufacturer and space-equipment supplier (CNES-supported), moving from subsystems to full smallsat solutions (EO, IR, space situational awareness).
companies.sovereignty_role: French sovereign smallsat manufacturer (e.g. Kineis IoT constellation, defence-relevant missions) within the European EO/SSA value chain.

key_investors: Management buyout of Nexeya space activities

uncertain: No public valuation/funding figure - size_eur null.

companies.name: PLD Space
companies.description: Spanish launch company (Elche) developing the MIURA 1 suborbital and MIURA 5 orbital rockets. Closed a EUR180m Series C in Mar 2026 (led by Mitsubishi Electric); ~EUR210m raised in 2026.
companies.sovereignty_role: Spain's sovereign launch contender for European autonomous access to space.
uncertain: Private (no BME listing yet despite IPO speculation); ~EUR210m raised used for sizing.
